fix(cancel): EMAIL_FROM statt EMAIL_USER als Absender-Adresse

EMAIL_USER ist der SMTP-Login, nicht die Absender-Adresse. Der SMTP-Server
hat die FROM-Adresse mit 501 rejected. Jetzt wird EMAIL_FROM verwendet,
genau wie im funktionierenden mailer.js Service.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add explicit find-vs-grep rule to sys1 prompt

Examples alone are not enough for a 1.5B model to reliably distinguish
filename search from content search. Add a two-line rule directly in
the prompt that spells out the distinction:

  File by NAME (called, named)         → find -iname
  File by CONTENT (contains word, has text inside) → grep -ril

Small models follow explicit rules more reliably than inferring them
from few-shot examples. This should fix recurring "contains word X"
queries being misrouted to find -iname instead of grep -ril.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fuse gate+up GEMV into single dispatch with split writer kernel

Reduces FFN chain from 6 to 5 programs per layer by combining gate and up
projections into a single GEMV that writes to two separate output buffers
via a split writer kernel. Saves one program boundary + one activation
read per layer × 32 layers (~1ms/tok improvement).

commit.message:
Fix Turn 2 ignoring valid search results + wrong command for content search

Root cause: find -iname searches FILENAMES not file contents. For "file
containing word X", grep -ril is correct. find -iname is for "file named X".

Changes:
- sys1: add grep example for Downloads to anchor "contains word X" → grep
  (previously only Desktop+Documents had grep examples; Downloads was missing,
  so LLM matched Downloads queries to find -iname instead)
- reformatOutput: add handlers for plain find (filename results) and grep -r
  (content results) — both add a labeled header so Turn 2 knows what the
  data represents instead of seeing raw unlabelled filenames
- sys2: rewrite from hedging ("if data doesn't answer, say I don't have it")
  to directive ("report what the data shows") — stops LLM from asking
  clarifying questions when it receives unlabelled output
- usr2: reinstate command context "Command output (from `cmd`):" so Turn 2
  always knows what command produced the data
- Side effect: header line bumps allLines count from 4→5, so clipboard
  copy now fires correctly for 4-result searches

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Suggest Edit and Copy Link interactive features

Select any text to get a floating toolbar with two options:

1. Suggest Edit — opens a modal to write a suggested change, then
   submits it as a structured GitHub Issue (no auth or git knowledge
   needed). Issue includes original text, suggestion, section context.

2. Copy Link — generates a scroll-to-text-fragment URL (#:~:text=)
   and copies to clipboard. Recipients get the text auto-highlighted
   and scrolled to when they open the link.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Crawl action items in cmd_crawl with 60-day lookback

Previously cmd_crawl only extracted birthdays. Action items were only
crawled during the daily digest with a 3-day window, so the bot only
ever had this week's items. Now:
- cmd_crawl does both birthdays (2yr) and action items (60 days)
- Daily digest crawl widened from 3 to 14 days for ongoing coverage
- Blocked sender filtering applied to both pipelines

fix: resume-from-checkpoint + persist 429 state across restarts

Problem: container restart always re-fetches same accounts (insertion order),
@sama first → immediate 429 → 200+ accounts never reached.

Fix 1 (db.py): ORDER BY last_fetched_at ASC NULLS FIRST
- NEVER-fetched accounts always go first
- After restart, accounts already done this cycle sort to back naturally

Fix 2 (db.py + scheduler.py): persist rate_limit_reset_at in scraper_state
- On 429: write reset timestamp to DB, clear after normal sleep
- On startup: if reset window still active, sleep remaining time first
- Log: [CYCLE] Rate limit carry-over from previous run, sleeping Xs

commit.message:
fix(smith): Copilot reads prompt from piped stdin without -p flag

Copilot CLI's -p flag only accepts literal text, not stdin via -p -.
Omitting -p entirely makes Copilot read from piped stdin and run
non-interactively, matching Claude's -p - behavior. This also avoids
the Windows 32K command-line length limit that would be hit with large
prompts (e.g. Fhi.Metadata has 56KB of context files alone).

commit.message:
fix(smith): Copilot CLI does not support stdin prompt via -p -

Copilot CLI's -p flag only accepts literal text, not stdin. The smith
was passing -p - which copilot interpreted as the prompt being a dash
character. Now copilot prompts are passed directly as -p <text> via
BuildArgsWithPrompt, while claude/gemini continue using stdin.

commit.message:
fix(channel): upsert device to PostgreSQL before creating channel account

provision-device FK fix: devices registered via /api/device/register
only exist in memory until a bind occurs. channel_accounts has a FK
constraint to devices(device_id), so provision-device fails for fresh
test devices. Ensure device is saved to PostgreSQL before INSERT.

commit.message:
Fix: content search on Desktop misrouted to filename search

Add grep example for Desktop to anchor "has the word X in it" queries
to grep -ril instead of find -iname. The LLM had two Desktop examples
both using find -iname, and the only grep example said "in documents"
not "on desktop" — so it always pattern-matched Desktop → find.

Also: yes, 17 files on your Desktop contain the word "language"
(wp_fb.html, bookmarks_10_04_2021.html, a.html, and others).

Day 20: Closed-world reasoning, copilot tools, visibility scoping

- Rewrite reasoning system prompt for closed-world model: evidence-only
  reasoning, governance policies as hard blockers, evidence-based output
  schema (evidenceSummary, evidenceFor/evidenceAgainst)
- Add post-reasoning policy verification in reasoning engine to catch
  LLM proposing blocked/unpermitted actions, override to null
- Query Operator record for company name in reasoning prompt
- Add get_operational_briefing copilot tool: department-grouped situation
  summary with period filter (today/week/month)
- Add search_department_knowledge copilot tool: RAG search across visible
  department documents with graceful fallback
- Rewrite get_department_context tool: fix digital entity count via
  department-member relationships, show active situations
- Thread visibleDepts through all copilot tool executions; scope
  get_org_structure, list_departments, create_situation_type
- Update situations page: evidence summary display, backward-compatible
  rendering for old pros/cons and new evidenceFor/evidenceAgainst formats

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix video/file-type search and ls sort header bug

- Add sys1 examples for finding videos/images across device using find -ls
  with proper extension grouping and sort -k7 -rn (macOS-compatible)
- Add reformatOutput handler for find -ls output: parses inode/size/path
  columns and converts byte counts to human-readable (K/M/G), handles
  filenames with spaces via f[10...].joined()
- Fix -S flag detection in ls reformatOutput: cmd.contains("-S") was false
  for combined flags like -lhS (no literal "-S" substring). Now extracts
  the flags token and checks for uppercase S within it
- Fix Swift escape: \( in triple-quoted string was parsed as interpolation;
  changed to \\( / \\) to produce literal shell \( \) in the prompt
- Fix sort flag: sort -rh is GNU-only; use sort -rn for macOS compatibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Jenkins validation pipeline support (v0.15.0)

New variables: validation_jenkins_job, backend_repo_url, backend_image_name,
backend_jenkins_job, frontend_jenkins_job, validation_image_name,
validation_credential_id. When validation_jenkins_job is set:
- Jenkinsfile builds without :latest, archives metadata, triggers validation
- Jenkinsfile.validation runs tests in K8s Job, promotes images on success
- Optional credential injection for test secrets (S3, OIDC, etc.)
- Dockerfile.validation and validation-entrypoint.sh for test container

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(channel): send ECLAW_READY init push to bot after full bind

On successful channel bind, immediately fire-and-forget an ECLAW_READY
system push containing deviceId/entityId/botSecret credentials and
pre-built curl templates for Mission Dashboard tools, so the bot has
full context without waiting for a user message.

commit.message:
Delete webhook on bot startup to fix long-polling not receiving updates

Telegram silently ignores getUpdates when a webhook is set. Call
deleteWebhook at the start of the polling loop to ensure compatibility.

repository.description: Connect a gameserver to your tf2pickup.org instance

commit.message:
fix: install slash commands to ~/.claude/commands/ instead of ~/.claude/skills/

Claude Code only recognizes slash commands in ~/.claude/commands/ (user-level)
or .claude/commands/ (project-level). The old ~/.claude/skills/ path was
invisible to Claude Code, so /tmux-* commands never appeared.

Also updates web-install.sh to use the same correct path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: explicitly set --model opus for workers to prevent Sonnet fallback

When booting 10+ workers simultaneously, some fail to resolve the Max
subscription and fall back to Sonnet on API billing. Adding --model opus
ensures all workers use the correct model.

commit.message:
Day 20: Category-aware context assembly, department RAG, scope simplification

- Rewrite context-assembly.ts: replace BFS traversal with category-aware
  department discovery, RAG retrieval, and category-grouped related entities
- Restructure reasoning-prompts.ts: department context, RAG knowledge chunks,
  grouped related entities (People/Operational Data/External Parties)
- Simplify situation-scope.ts: replace 10-hop BFS with direct department
  checks per entity category (parentDepartmentId / department-member rels)
- Fix orientation-prompts.ts: digital entity count via department-member
  relationships instead of parentDepartmentId; external count via member hops

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Telegram bot silent failures from unescaped HTML in responses

HTML-escape all DB-sourced strings in chat handler to prevent Telegram
API 400 errors. Add plain-text fallback retry in send_message so users
always get a response even if markup is malformed.
